title: Scotland Allocates Â£3.4 Million to 11 Green Hydrogen Projects
content: The Scottish Government has committed Â£3.4 million in match-funding grants to 11 projects ranging from Orkney to Shetland and the mainland to accelerate green hydrogen production, strengthen the supply chain, and expand transport and storage infrastructure. Acting Net Zero Secretary Gillian Martin highlighted hydrogenâ€™s pivotal role in reaching net zero by 2045 and delivering a just transition that leverages Scotlandâ€™s industrial heritage, skilled workforce, and natural resources. Grant awards, capped at 50% funding up to Â£2 million per project, followed a two-stage selection process that shortlisted 18 proposals before finalizing the successful 11.
author: Analysts Account
